story.text: As the loop owner, I need the shape of the fleet overseer agreed before it is built, because the repo now runs a real fleet (14 open PRs, parallel sessions) with no single part watching the whole of it, and the failure mode is rushing a powerful agent-launcher to main before its authority, governance, and second-truth risks are settled. This atom carries the BLUEPRINT only: a three-tier model (Administrator oversees the fleet and launches per-unit Conductors; a Conductor drives one unit and launches worker agents; agents do the work), framed as a projection plus a governed loop that never becomes a second authority and never launches past an admitted dial. It proposes; it builds nothing. The owner steers the name (Administrator vs Controller), the frame, and the authority dial; only then does the first read-only increment (loop/fleet.py) get built.

briefing.headline: Agree the fleet-overseer blueprint (the Administrator) before building any organ
briefing.value: A single, glance-able account of how ontum should oversee all its tasks as a mesh and launch agents against them, grounded in organs that already exist (the spawn rail as the launcher; herald, trust, and the inference queue as the mesh and its governance; digest, census, heal, gaps, activity, and watcher as the per-axis sensors). The blueprint names the one part that is missing: the Administrator that reads the fleet whole and dispatches the right governed launch per unit, with owner-gated items escalated, never auto-launched.
briefing.why_now: The fleet is already real and ungoverned-as-a-whole: tonight a session hand-conducted exactly one unit end-to-end (the review queue) because nothing standing does it. Foundation-first (the blueprint-before-build rule): the hard calls (the Administrator's authority, how it stays a projection not a second authority, the holon-vs-gate-face overload) are cheaper to settle on paper than after a powerful launcher is on main.
briefing.if_accepted: The owner has a shape to steer: a name, a three-tier frame, and an authority dial. The first increment falls out as a read-only fleet-fold (loop/fleet.py) that maps every unit, agent, and block plus the one launch each needs, with nothing acting until the dial is set.
briefing.if_rejected: Fleet oversight stays ad-hoc and per-session; the owner keeps having to hold the whole board in his head; and the next powerful launcher risks being built before its governance is agreed.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low: this is a document. It proposes and builds nothing; every downstream organ is gated by the owner's confirmation of the frame and the dial (D-4). The blast radius of a wrong blueprint is a revised blueprint.
briefing.mechanism: administrator.proposal.md: the full shape, the three tiers, what already exists to compose, the Administrator's organs (the eyes loop/fleet.py, the hand the launch loop, the Conductor role, the authority dial), the separation-of-powers governance, the brokerage tie-in, a labelled concept list (C1-C7), and the calls to action.
